comment_text.sn13.11:1.2: Sineru, also known as Neru (Ja 379:3.1), Meru (DN 32:7.2), or Sumeru (Thi Ap 17:53.2), is the axis mundi, the mountain around which the world revolves. The oldest reference is perhaps Taittiriya Āraṇyaka 1.7.1.3. Kaśyapa, the eighth above the seven Sūryas, dwells constantly on Meru, never leaving, while the other Suns illuminate different regions. Sages have gone as far as the regions around, but none reached the mountain itself.

root_text.sn13.11:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso sinerussa pabbatarājassa satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn13.11:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yā vā satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā yo vā sineru pabbatarājā”ti?
root_text.sn13.11:2.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti sineruṁ pabbatarājānaṁ upanidhāya satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā”ti.
root_text.sn13.11:3.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa diṭṭhisampannassa puggalassa adhigamaṁ upanidhāya aññatitthiyasamaṇabrāhmaṇaparibbājakānaṁ adhigamo neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upeti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upeti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upeti.
root_text.sn13.11:3.2: Evaṁ mahādhigamo, bhikkhave, diṭṭhisampanno puggalo, evaṁ mahābhiñño”ti.

translation_text.sn13.11:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place on Sineru, the king of mountains, seven pebbles the size of mung beans.
translation_text.sn13.11:1.4: Which is more: the seven pebbles the size of mung beans, or Sineru, the king of mountains?”
translation_text.sn13.11:2.1: “Sir, Sineru, the king of mountains, is certainly more.
translation_text.sn13.11:2.2: The seven pebbles the size of mung beans are tiny.
translation_text.sn13.11:2.3: Compared to Sineru, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.”
translation_text.sn13.11:3.1: “In the same way, compared with the achievements of a noble disciple accomplished in view, the achievements of the ascetics, brahmins, and wanderers of other religions is not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.
translation_text.sn13.11:3.2: So great is the achievement of the individual accomplished in view, so great is their direct knowledge.”
translation_text.sn13.11:3.4: The Linked Discourses on comprehension are complete.
